jungfraujoch-top-of-europe.name: Jungfraujoch – Top of Europe
jungfraujoch-top-of-europe.description: Visit the highest railway station in Europe at 3,454m with stunning views of the Aletsch Glacier.
jungfraujoch-top-of-europe.longDescription: The Jungfraujoch – Top of Europe is one of Switzerland's most iconic destinations. Take the cogwheel train through the Eiger to reach the highest railway station in Europe at 3,454 meters. Marvel at the panoramic views of the Aletsch Glacier, visit the Ice Palace carved deep into the glacier, and enjoy the Alpine Sensation exhibition. On clear days, you can see as far as the Black Forest in Germany and the Vosges in France.

zermatt-matterhorn-glacier-paradise.name: Matterhorn Glacier Paradise

zermatt-matterhorn-glacier-paradise.longDescription: Ascend to 3,883 meters on Europe's highest cable car station. The Matterhorn Glacier Paradise offers a 360° panoramic platform with views of 38 four-thousand-meter peaks across Switzerland, Italy, and France. Inside, explore the Glacier Palace with its ice sculptures and crystal chamber. In winter and spring, enjoy year-round skiing on the glacier.

swiss-museum-of-transport.longDescription: Located in Lucerne, the Swiss Museum of Transport is the country's most popular museum. Explore over 3,000 exhibits covering road transport, railways, aviation, and space travel. The museum features a planetarium, a Swiss Chocolate Adventure, a film theatre, and an interactive media world. Perfect for families and anyone fascinated by how Switzerland moves.

lake-geneva-boat-cruise.name: Lake Geneva Boat Cruise
lake-geneva-boat-cruise.description: Scenic boat cruise on Lake Geneva passing vineyards, castles, and the stunning Alpine backdrop.
lake-geneva-boat-cruise.longDescription: Cruise the crystal-clear waters of Lake Geneva aboard a historic Belle Époque paddle steamer or modern vessel. Pass the UNESCO-listed Lavaux vineyards, the iconic Jet d'Eau, Château de Chillon, and charming lakeside towns. Choose from short 1-hour cruises to full-day excursions. The lake offers stunning views of the French Alps and Mont Blanc on clear days.

titlis-glacier-excursion.description: Ride the world's first revolving cable car to the glacier summit at 3,238m.
titlis-glacier-excursion.longDescription: Mount Titlis offers one of Central Switzerland's most spectacular mountain experiences. Take the world's first revolving gondola — the Titlis Rotair — to the summit at 3,238 meters. Walk across Europe's highest suspension bridge, the Titlis Cliff Walk, explore the Glacier Cave, and enjoy year-round snow activities on the glacier.

chateau-de-chillon.name: Château de Chillon
chateau-de-chillon.description: Switzerland's most visited castle, a medieval fortress on the shores of Lake Geneva.
chateau-de-chillon.longDescription: Château de Chillon is a fairy-tale castle sitting on a rocky island on the shores of Lake Geneva near Montreux. Dating back to the 12th century, this medieval fortress features 25 buildings and 3 courtyards. Explore the underground vaults, grand halls, and towers with panoramic lake views. The castle inspired Lord Byron's poem 'The Prisoner of Chillon'.

interlaken-paragliding.longDescription: Experience the thrill of tandem paragliding over one of Switzerland's most beautiful valleys. Launch from Beatenberg or Amisbühl and soar above Interlaken with views of Lake Thun, Lake Brienz, and the Jungfrau massif. No experience needed — professional pilots handle everything. Flights last 15-20 minutes with optional acrobatic maneuvers.

grindelwald-first-cliff-walk.longDescription: Grindelwald First offers a full day of mountain thrills. Take the gondola to First (2,168m) and walk the spectacular Cliff Walk — a narrow pathway along the cliff face with a viewing platform extending over the abyss. Then experience the First Flyer zip line, First Glider eagle-ride, and Trottibike scooter descent. Mountain Cart and hiking trails round out the experience.

rhine-falls-schaffhausen.name: Rhine Falls
rhine-falls-schaffhausen.description: Europe's largest waterfall — a thundering spectacle near Schaffhausen.
rhine-falls-schaffhausen.longDescription: The Rhine Falls near Schaffhausen is Europe's largest plain waterfall, 150 meters wide and 23 meters high. Take a boat ride right up to the central rock in the middle of the falls, or enjoy the viewing platforms along both banks. The adventure park nearby offers zip lines and a children's playground. Evening illuminations in summer add a magical touch.

laederach-chocolate-factory.longDescription: Discover the art of Swiss chocolate-making at Läderach's ChocoAtelier in Bilten. The guided tour takes you through the chocolate production process, from cocoa bean to finished praline. Includes generous tastings of premium Swiss chocolate and a workshop where you create your own chocolate bar. A sweet experience that captures Switzerland's chocolate heritage.

bern-old-town-walking-tour.longDescription: Discover Bern's beautifully preserved medieval old town, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Walk under 6km of covered arcades, see the Zytglogge clock tower's astronomical clock, visit the Bear Park, and enjoy views from the Rosengarten. The tour covers the Einstein House, the Gothic cathedral, and the stunning Federal Palace.

lauterbrunnen-valley-hike.name: Lauterbrunnen Valley Waterfalls Hike
lauterbrunnen-valley-hike.description: Hike through the valley of 72 waterfalls, including the 300m Staubbach Falls.
lauterbrunnen-valley-hike.longDescription: Lauterbrunnen Valley is one of the most dramatic landscapes in the Alps, featuring 72 waterfalls cascading from sheer cliffs. The easy valley hike takes you past the iconic 300-meter Staubbach Falls, through charming villages, and to the Trümmelbach Falls — the only underground glacier waterfalls in Europe accessible by lift. J.R.R. Tolkien's inspiration for Rivendell.

zurich-kunsthaus.longDescription: The Kunsthaus Zürich is one of the most important art museums in Switzerland and Europe. The collection spans from the Middle Ages to contemporary art, featuring works by Monet, Picasso, Chagall, Giacometti, and the world's largest Munch collection outside Norway. The new extension by David Chipperfield doubled the exhibition space in 2021.

glacier-express.longDescription: The Glacier Express is the world's most famous panoramic train ride, connecting Zermatt to St. Moritz in approximately 8 hours. Cross 291 bridges, pass through 91 tunnels, and traverse the Oberalp Pass at 2,033 meters. The panoramic windows offer unobstructed views of dramatic gorges, lush valleys, and towering peaks.

curling-zermatt.name: Curling Experience Zermatt
curling-zermatt.description: Try Olympic curling on outdoor ice rinks with Matterhorn views in Zermatt.
curling-zermatt.longDescription: Experience the Olympic sport of curling in one of the world's most dramatic settings — outdoor ice rinks in Zermatt with the Matterhorn as your backdrop. Professional instructors teach the basics of stone delivery, sweeping, and strategy in a fun group session.

providers.name: Zermatt Curling Club
providers.description: 2-hour beginner session with equipment

marketplaces.description: Switzerland's biggest activities marketplace — verified suppliers, free cancellation

tobogganing-rigi.name: Tobogganing on Rigi
tobogganing-rigi.description: Sled down a scenic 6km toboggan run on the 'Queen of the Mountains' above Lake Lucerne.
tobogganing-rigi.longDescription: The Rigi toboggan run is a spectacular 6km sled descent from Rigi Kulm to Klösterli through snowy alpine scenery above Lake Lucerne. Take the cogwheel railway up and sled down — a thrilling winter experience combining mountain views with adrenaline.

providers.name: Rigi Bahnen (Sled + Railway)
providers.description: Cogwheel railway up + sled rental

marketplaces.description: Switzerland's biggest activities marketplace — verified suppliers, free cancellation

fat-biking-gstaad.name: Fat Biking Gstaad
fat-biking-gstaad.description: Ride oversized-tire bikes through snowy Gstaad trails for a unique winter cycling experience.
fat-biking-gstaad.longDescription: Fat biking on groomed winter trails around Gstaad is a unique way to explore the snowy landscape. The extra-wide tires grip packed snow effortlessly as you ride through forests, along frozen rivers, and past traditional chalets in the Saanenland.

providers.name: Gstaad Fat Bike Rental
providers.description: Half-day fat bike rental

providers.name: Gstaad Fat Bike Guided Tour
providers.description: 2-hour guided fat bike tour with fondue stop

marketplaces.description: Switzerland's biggest activities marketplace — verified suppliers, free cancellation

cave-tour-holloch.name: Hölloch Cave Tour
cave-tour-holloch.description: Explore one of the world's longest cave systems — 200+ km of underground passages in Schwyz.
cave-tour-holloch.longDescription: The Hölloch in Muotathal is one of the longest cave systems in the world with over 200km of explored passages. Guided tours range from beginner-friendly 3-hour walks to multi-day expeditions deep underground through narrow passages, underground rivers, and vast cathedral-like chambers.

providers.name: Trekking Team Hölloch (Beginner)
providers.description: 3-hour introductory cave tour

providers.name: Trekking Team Hölloch (Full Day)
providers.description: 7-hour deep cave expedition

marketplaces.description: Switzerland's biggest activities marketplace — verified suppliers, free cancellation

gorner-gorge-zermatt.name: Gorner Gorge Zermatt
gorner-gorge-zermatt.description: Walk through a dramatic glacier gorge carved by meltwater from the Gorner Glacier.
gorner-gorge-zermatt.longDescription: The Gornerschlucht is a dramatic gorge carved over millennia by meltwater from the Gorner Glacier above Zermatt. Wooden walkways and galleries lead through the narrow chasm with walls up to 50m high, thundering waterfalls, and turquoise glacier water rushing below.

marketplaces.description: Switzerland's biggest activities marketplace — verified suppliers, free cancellation

blausee-lake.name: Blausee – Blue Lake
blausee-lake.description: A magical crystal-clear blue lake in an enchanted forest park near Kandersteg.
blausee-lake.longDescription: Blausee (Blue Lake) is a small but stunning crystal-clear lake with an intense blue colour, nestled in an enchanting forest park in the Kandertal. The spring-fed lake is so clear you can see every detail of the bottom. A nature park with trout farm, spa, and restaurant surrounds the lake.

marketplaces.description: Switzerland's biggest activities marketplace — verified suppliers, free cancellation

caumasee-flims.name: Caumasee – Lake Cauma
caumasee-flims.description: A hidden emerald-green swimming lake in the forest above Flims — one of Switzerland's gems.
caumasee-flims.longDescription: Caumasee (Lake Cauma) near Flims is a hidden gem — a stunning emerald-green lake surrounded by forest, accessible by a lift tunnel carved into the rock. The geothermally warmed water reaches a comfortable 24°C in summer, making it one of the warmest swimming lakes in the region.

providers.name: Caumasee (Day Entry)
providers.description: Day access including lift tunnel

marketplaces.description: Switzerland's biggest activities marketplace — verified suppliers, free cancellation

oeschinen-lake-winter.name: Oeschinen Lake Winter Experience
oeschinen-lake-winter.description: Visit the frozen UNESCO lake for ice fishing, winter hiking, and a magical snow-covered landscape.
oeschinen-lake-winter.longDescription: Lake Oeschinen transforms in winter into a magical frozen wonderland. When conditions allow, the frozen surface becomes a natural ice rink and ice fishing spot. Winter hiking trails through the snow-covered landscape and a toboggan run from the gondola station complete the experience.

providers.name: Oeschinensee Gondola (Winter)
providers.description: Return gondola + toboggan run included

marketplaces.description: Switzerland's biggest activities marketplace — verified suppliers, free cancellation

klontalersee.name: Klöntalersee
klontalersee.description: A serene alpine lake in canton Glarus with turquoise waters, free access, and mountain backdrop.
klontalersee.longDescription: Klöntalersee is a beautiful alpine lake in the Klöntal valley of canton Glarus. The turquoise-green waters reflect the surrounding Glärnisch massif. A popular but uncrowded spot for swimming, fishing, and walking, with free shore access and car-free zones.

marketplaces.description: Switzerland's biggest activities marketplace — verified suppliers, free cancellation

thermal-baths-yverdon.name: Thermal Baths Yverdon-les-Bains
thermal-baths-yverdon.description: Natural sulphur thermal springs in the lakeside town of Yverdon-les-Bains.
thermal-baths-yverdon.longDescription: The Centre Thermal Yverdon-les-Bains draws on natural sulphur-rich hot springs that have been known since Roman times. Indoor and outdoor pools, a fitness pool, massage jets, and a wellness area. Set on the shores of Lake Neuchâtel with views of the Jura mountains.

providers.name: Centre Thermal (2 hours)
providers.description: 2-hour pool access

providers.name: Centre Thermal (Full Day)
providers.description: Full-day access with wellness area

solothurn-old-town.name: Solothurn Old Town Tour
solothurn-old-town.description: Explore the 'most beautiful baroque city in Switzerland' — the city built on the number 11.
solothurn-old-town.longDescription: Solothurn is renowned as Switzerland's finest baroque city, built around the number 11 — 11 churches, 11 chapels, 11 towers, and 11 fountains. The beautifully preserved old town features the magnificent St. Ursen Cathedral, colourful Renaissance fountains, and the Zeitglockenturm.

providers.name: Self-guided Walk (Free)
providers.description: Free self-guided walk through old town

providers.name: Guided Old Town Tour
providers.description: 90-minute guided walking tour

aarau-old-town.name: Aarau Old Town & Painted Eaves
aarau-old-town.description: Discover Aarau's unique painted roof eaves (Dachhimmel) — the most beautiful in Switzerland.
aarau-old-town.longDescription: Aarau's old town is famous for its Dachhimmel — elaborately painted eaves (roof undersides) dating from the 16th century, unique in Switzerland. Walk through the medieval streets admiring these colourful artworks while exploring the cantonal capital with its Aare riverside setting and boutique shops.

travel-tips-switzerland.name: Plan your trip to Switzerland
travel-tips-switzerland.description: Switzerland is a very diverse country and has an incredible amount to offer for your trip. So don't let the small area fool you. Although Switzerland is around ten times smaller than Sweden, California or Morocco, you will find something for every taste. In Mediterranean Ticino you are in a completely different world than in busy Basel. And the mountainous Alpstein has a very different climate than the mild Lake Geneva region. And this, although these places are only three to four hours apart. In our blog we help you with your travel planning. We provide you with helpful travel tips and useful information so that you can prepare perfectly. It doesn't matter whether you're traveling to Switzerland for the first time or already know your way around. Our articles are suitable for first-time visitors as well as for real Switzerland connoisseurs.
